title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)
label: mx1_transmute_11
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Problem:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
The problem is reformulated into a combinatorial expectation framework suitable for computational solving.
node_root_helper_01 (transmuted_child): Computational Plan for Random Chord Region Expectation: 1. Discrete Combinatorial Framework: Define the set of chords C = {c1, c2, ..., c25}. Each chord ci is defined by two endpoints (ui, vi) located in distinct quadrants. Let Q = {1, 2, 3, 4} be the s
final_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 26 + 300 * P(intersection)
JUSTIFICATION: Expected regions equals 1 plus chords plus expected intersections.

run_level_variable_overrides.selected_prompt_text:
Reformulate the provided problem into a structured computational plan. First, translate the geometric constraints into a discrete combinatorial framework (e.g., defining the probability of intersection for a pair of elements based on their endpoint locations). Second, express the total expected value as a sum of indicator variables using the linearity of expectation. Third, outline a verification step where the agent must check the consistency of the intersection probability against the specific constraints provided. Use plain text only inside the message. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or LaTeX-style backslashes.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
